data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-cd69c24a">
    <title>agent: honor triaged operator overrides in readiness</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
After an operator reviews a proposed bead and promotes it back to open, pre-claim readiness can evaluate the same finding again and park the bead back to proposed. That creates a downgrade loop and contradicts the operator intent to keep work moving.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md:70-75 already defines proposed -> open as operator-driven triaged, but cli/internal/bead/store.go:587-628 status transitions mutate lifecycle metadata without necessarily appending a durable triaged event that readiness can query.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1207-1226 unconditionally parks terminal readiness findings without checking for a later operator triage/acceptance.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 stores intake.blocked without a stable fingerprint, so even if an operator override exists the loop cannot tell whether the current readiness finding is the same one.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reuse the existing TD-027 triaged semantics for proposed -> open. Ensure operator promotion records a durable signal: either a triaged event or an explicit operator-accepted marker attached to the status transition event body.
- Add a stable readiness finding fingerprint for parkable intake findings. Include at least the decision class/rule, normalized reason, and a hash of prompt-relevant bead fields used to determine whether the bead changed since operator acceptance.
- Before parking an open bead for pre-claim readiness, check whether a later proposed -> open operator acceptance covers the same fingerprint. If yes, append/emit override-honored evidence and proceed instead of parking.
- If prompt-relevant fields changed after the operator acceptance, allow readiness to re-evaluate and park with a new fingerprint.

NON-SCOPE
- No broad WARN/BLOCK policy changes; those are in ddx-91898f19.
- No new event vocabulary unless the spec/observability bead authorizes it.
- No changes to closed/reopen semantics for closed -> open.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7. Depends on ddx-ef9e8aec.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Add or update TestLifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged to prove operator status update proposed -> open leaves durable triaged/operator-acceptance evidence visible to the readiness loop.
2. Add TestPreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking: seed a bead that was parked for readiness, promote it proposed -> open, run the same readiness finding again, and assert status remains open or proceeds to claim instead of proposed.
3. Add TestPreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride: after the operator promotion, mutate prompt-relevant bead text/AC and prove a new hard operator_required finding may park with a new fingerprint.
4. Production wiring is covered: the override check runs before every pre-claim ParkToProposed call in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, not only in a test helper.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(LifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged|PreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking|PreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride)"` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Operator reviewed pre-claim parking on 2026-05-12. Bead is execution-ready as written; readiness rewrite dropped the NON-SCOPE commitment that broad WARN/BLOCK policy changes belong to ddx-91898f19. Preserve the original prompt and skip lifecycle rewrite for execution.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:bead-lifecycle, area:beads, kind:bug, prevention, spec:FEAT-010, td:027, td:031, triage:skip</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-cc0641a7" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="68949ae0f78cbe2b11753483f1ab10610e07749c" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T180410-dfb1da01"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and verified.

What changed:
- `proposed -> open` now leaves durable `triaged` evidence in the bead store, including the accepted readiness fingerprint and prompt fingerprint, so readiness can see operator acceptance later. See [store.go:587](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/bead/store.go#L587) and [lifecycle_transition_store_test.go:76](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/bead/lifecycle_transition_store_test.go#L76).
- Added a stable prompt fingerprint helper in [fingerprint.go:1](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/bead/fingerprint.go#L1).
- Pre-claim parking now fingerprints the readiness finding, checks for a later matching triage override, and skips repeat parking when the operator already accepted the same finding. See [execute_bead_loop.go:2897](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go#L2897) and [preclaim_intake_override.go:1](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_override.go#L1).
- Added regression coverage for repeat parking and changed prompt invalidation in [execute_bead_intake_test.go:347](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cd69c24a-20260512T180410-dfb1da01/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go#L347).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "Test(LifecycleProposedToOpenAppendsTriaged|PreClaimIntakeOperatorOverridePreventsRepeatParking|PreClaimIntakeChangedBeadInvalidatesOverride)"` passed.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...` passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed.

Commits:
- `fc71e218c` `fix(bead): honor triaged operator overrides in readiness [ddx-cd69c24a]`
- `9ecb00a79` `fix(bead): add prompt fingerprint helper [ddx-cd69c24a]`

Working tree is clean.
